title: rumex bucephalophorus subsp. graecus
intro: Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. graecus, also known as bitter dock or Greek dock, is a subspecies of perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. graecus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
